title: Commentary on Key Financial Performance – Q1'26
content:
The credit union financial position continues to be supported by exceptionally strong capital levels. The credit union reports a net worth ratio of 16.60%, representing a decrease of 34.32 bps from the prior period, and a solvency ratio of 1.20. This robust buffer provides ample room for absorbing potential losses while maintaining regulatory standing.

Asset quality remains stable and manageable. The delinquency ratio stands at 0.00% of total loans, while net charge-offs stand at 0.00% of average loans. Delinquency has declined to -394.36 bps from the prior period, reflecting a continued absence of non-performing assets that requires attention only if trends shift.

Profitability remains positive. The credit union reports a return on average assets (ROAA) of 1.45% and a net interest margin of 2.49%, increased to 160.56 bps respectively, from the prior period. Improved earning-asset performance drove this expansion in margins. Net operating expenses to average assets decreased to -2.36%, supporting overall profitability.

Balance-sheet structure continues to reflect a conservative operating profile. Loans represent 19.48% of shares, while loan balances increased to 11.30%, and shares and deposits grew by 12.20%, reflecting continued balance-sheet expansion. The core funding ratio stands at 10.96%, demonstrating strong liquidity direction.

Overall, the credit union enters Q1'26 with solid capital adequacy, healthy profitability growth, and stable asset quality underpinning its operations. Its primary strength lies in maintaining a high net worth buffer despite recent regulatory adjustments. Sustaining performance will depend on preserving this funding discipline while monitoring emerging loan trends.
